name: Sourcegraph API
what_it_does: Universal code search and intelligence API that enables cross-repository symbol navigation, code search, and powers the Cody AI coding assistant.
use_cases: Cross-repository code search at scale for agents doing codebase analysis
Symbol and definition lookup across monorepos and polyrepos
Finding all usages of a function or type across thousands of repositories
Powering AI coding assistants with precise code context (Cody)
Code intelligence integrations for editors and CI/CD pipelines
not_for: Simple single-repository search (GitHub native search is sufficient)
Teams that cannot host or pay for a Sourcegraph instance
best_when: An agent needs to search, navigate, or understand code across many repositories, especially in large engineering organizations.
avoid_when: Your codebase is small and hosted on a single platform with adequate native search.

auth.notes: Personal access tokens for individual users; OAuth apps for integrations. Site admins can configure SAML/OIDC SSO. Token-based auth is the standard path for agent use.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free cloud plan with limited repositories; Sourcegraph.com free for public repos
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise self-hosted or cloud with custom pricing; Cody Pro at $9/user/month
pricing.notes: Open source Sourcegraph can be self-hosted for free. Cloud plans have a free tier. Enterprise pricing requires a contract.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by plan; GraphQL complexity limits enforced server-side

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL errors are returned in a standard errors array with message and locations; query complexity errors are clear. Code-intelligence errors can be opaque.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All standard GraphQL queries are idempotent reads; mutations are deterministic given the same input.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL query complexity limits can be hit by agents constructing deep or broad queries; use query cost analysis
Self-hosted instances may be behind firewalls; agents must be configured for the correct base URL per deployment
Repository syncing lag means recently pushed code may not be indexed immediately
Cody API is separate from the core Sourcegraph GraphQL API with different auth paths
No REST API — agents must work with GraphQL, increasing query construction complexity

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Token scoping supported. SOC2 certified for cloud offering. Self-hosted deployments inherit the security posture of the operator. Open source codebase allows audit.
